title: Streblus heterophyllus
taxonomy: Streblus heterophyllus is classified as a species of evergreen tree in the family Moraceae. It is a member of the genus Streblus, which is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The species Streblus heterophyllus is also known by its common name, Calvary tree, and is widely distributed across the Indo-Pacific region. The tree's classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics, including its unique leaf shapes and growth patterns. As a member of the Moraceae family, Streblus heterophyllus is closely related to other trees such as the figs and mulberries.
uses: Streblus heterophyllus, commonly known as the Indian fig or saraca, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are said to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In some cultures, the plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Streblus heterophyllus for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Streblus heterophyllus, also known as the Milin tree, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The leaves and bark of the tree are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling. Additionally, the tree is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of infections and wounds. properties: Streblus heterophyllus, also known as the Black Bead Tree, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its bark, leaves, and roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. constituents: Streblus heterophyllus contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant's active constituents also include saponins, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Additionally, Streblus heterophyllus has been found to contain essential oils, including terpenoids and sesquiterpenes. These compounds are believed to contribute to the plant's antimicrobial and antiparasitic properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the range and potential applications of Streblus heterophyllus' active constituents.

side_effects: Streblus heterophyllus is also known as the Kanjikallu tree or Kanjivaram tree, but information about its side effects is limited. intro: Streblus heterophyllus, also known as the Pagodawood or Copperleaf, is a tropical plant species native to the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique leaves and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Streblus heterophyllus include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Streblus heterophyllus is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health conditions, and its extracts have also been used in modern pharmaceutical applications.
